meaning: An exciting or unusual experience

meaning: Very pleasing to look at

example: I need to pack my school bag before I go to bed.

meaning: To cover the surface of something with paint
example: We are going to paint the walls of our classroom light blue.

meaning: To bring a vehicle to a halt and leave it temporarily
example: Dad had to park the car in the basement carpark.

meaning: To coat with paste and stick
example: Cut out the pictures and paste them into your scrapbook.

meaning: To interrupt action or speech briefly
example: The teacher paused the video so we could take notes.

meaning: To give someone money that is due for work done, goods received, or a debt incurred
example: I will use my allowance to pay for the chicken rice.

meaning: To remove the outer covering or skin from a fruit or vegetable
example: You need to peel the banana before eating it.

meaning: To take hold of and remove a flower, fruit, or vegetable from where it is growing
example: Please do not pick the flowers in the garden.

meaning: To decide on and arrange in advance
example: We need to plan our route for the treasure hunt.

meaning: To place a seed, bulb, or plant in the ground so that it can grow
example: We will plant some sunflower seeds in the garden today.

meaning: To direct someone's attention towards something by extending one's finger
example: It is rude to point your finger at people.

meaning: To flow rapidly in a steady stream
example: Carefully pour the hot water into the cup.

meaning: To perform an activity or exercise regularly in order to improve or maintain one's proficiency
example: You need to practice playing the piano every day.

meaning: To make something ready for use or consideration

meaning: To speak and act so as to make it appear that something is the case when in fact it is not
example: The children love to pretend they are brave knights.

meaning: To produce books, newspapers, or magazines, especially in large quantities, by a mechanical process involving the transfer of text, images, or designs to paper
example: Can you print this worksheet from the computer for me?

meaning: To exert force on someone or something so as to cause movement towards oneself
example: You have to pull the door hard to open it.

meaning: To strike with the fist
example: The boxer will punch the heavy bag during training.

meaning: To inflict a penalty or sanction on someone as retribution for an offense
example: The teacher had to punish the student for fighting.

meaning: To exert force on someone or something in order to move them away from oneself
example: Please do not push your friends when lining up.

meaning: To stretch out an arm in a specified direction in order to touch or grasp something
example: I am too short to reach the books on the top shelf.

meaning: To say something in response to something someone has said
example: Please reply to my question as soon as possible.

meaning: To give a spoken or written account of something that one has observed, heard, done, or investigated
example: You must report the lost wallet to the police.

meaning: To save someone from a dangerous or distressing situation
example: The brave lifeguard swam out to rescue the drowning boy.

meaning: To come or go back to a place or person
example: I must return these books to the library by Friday.

meaning: A full, deep, prolonged cry uttered by a lion or other large wild animal
example: We could hear the lion roar loudly from a distance.

meaning: To apply firm pressure to the surface of something, using a repeated back and forth motion
example: Do not rub your eyes if they are itchy.

meaning: One of a set of explicit or understood regulations or principles governing conduct within a particular activity or sphere
example: The most important rule in class is to respect others.

meaning: To move with urgent haste
example: We had to rush to the MRT station so we wouldn't be late.

meaning: To keep safe or rescue from harm or danger
example: Superman arrived just in time to save the day.

meaning: To gain a point or points in a competitive game or match
example: He managed to score a goal in the final minute of the soccer match.

meaning: To score or mark the surface of something with a sharp or pointed object
example: Be careful not to scratch the shiny table with your keys.

meaning: To give a long, loud, piercing cry or cries expressing excitement, great emotion, or pain
example: The roller coaster drop made everyone scream in excitement.

meaning: To try to find something by looking or otherwise seeking carefully and thoroughly
example: We need to search the whole room to find my lost keys.

meaning: To present food or drink to someone
example: The waiter will serve our food soon.

meaning: To resolve or reach an agreement about an argument or problem
example: They finally managed to settle their differences and become friends again.

meaning: To move irregularly and quickly up and down or from side to side
example: The dog will shake itself dry after a bath.

meaning: To give out a bright light
example: The sun will shine brightly after the rain stops.

meaning: To cause someone to feel surprised and upset
example: The sudden loud noise gave everyone a shock.

meaning: To fire a bullet from a gun or discharge an arrow from a bow
example: The archer aims carefully before he will shoot the arrow.

meaning: To utter a loud call or cry
example: Do not shout in the library; you must whisper.

meaning: To write one's name on a letter, card, or document to authorize or confirm it
example: Please sign your name at the bottom of the form.

meaning: To lose one's footing and slide unintentionally for a short distance
example: Be careful not to slip on the wet floor.

meaning: To perceive or detect the odor or scent of something
example: I love the smell of freshly baked bread.

meaning: To form one's features into a pleased, kind, or amused expression
example: She gave me a big smile when I gave her the present.

meaning: Vibrations that travel through the air or another medium and can be heard when they reach a person's or animal's ear
example: I heard a strange sound coming from the dark room.

meaning: To write or name the letters that form a word in correct sequence
example: Can you spell the word 'beautiful' correctly?

meaning: To cause or allow liquid to flow over the edge of its container, especially unintentionally
example: Be careful not to spill your milk on the table.

meaning: To diminish or destroy the value or quality of
example: Too much rain will spoil our plans for a picnic.

meaning: To apply liquid to someone or something in the form of a shower of tiny drops
example: Mother will spray some water on the plants.

meaning: To firmly press something soft or yielding, typically with one's fingers
example: You need to squeeze the lemon to get the juice out.

meaning: To look fixedly or vacantly at someone or something with one's eyes wide open
example: It is rude to stare at people you do not know.

meaning: To begin to do or be
example: The race will start when you hear the whistle.

meaning: To remain in the same place
example: Please stay here while I go and buy some drinks.

meaning: To take another person's property without permission or legal right and without intending to return it
example: It is wrong to steal things that do not belong to you.

meaning: To lift and set down one's foot or one foot after the other in order to walk somewhere or move to a new position
example: Watch your step when you walk down the stairs.

meaning: To pierce or wound painfully with a sharp-pointed structure or organ
example: If you disturb the bee, it might sting you.

meaning: To move a spoon or other implement around in a liquid or other substance in order to mix it thoroughly
example: Use a spoon to stir the sugar into your tea.

meaning: To come to an end; to cease to happen
example: The bus will stop at the traffic light.

meaning: To be made or be capable of being made longer or wider without tearing or breaking
example: Remember to stretch your muscles before exercising.

meaning: To hit forcibly and deliberately with one's hand or a weapon or other implement
example: The clock will strike twelve at midnight.

meaning: To make forceful or violent efforts to get free of restraint or constriction
example: The fly tried to struggle free from the spider's web.

meaning: To devote time and attention to acquiring knowledge on an academic subject, especially by means of books
example: I need to study for my science test tomorrow.

meaning: To take away a number or amount from another to calculate the difference
example: If you subtract two from five, you are left with three.

meaning: To achieve the desired aim or result
example: If you work hard, you will succeed in your goals.

meaning: To experience or be subjected to something bad or unpleasant
example: He had to suffer a stomachache after eating too much ice cream.

meaning: To make something needed or wanted available to someone; provide
example: The school will supply all the materials we need for art class.

meaning: An unexpected or astonishing event, fact, or thing
example: We threw a surprise birthday party for my sister.

meaning: To cause or allow something, especially food or drink, to pass down the throat
example: Drink some water to help you swallow the pill.

meaning: To clean an area by brushing away dirt or litter
example: Please sweep the floor after you accidentally drop the biscuits.

meaning: To become larger or rounder in size, typically as a result of an accumulation of fluid
example: His ankle began to swell after he twisted it.

meaning: To move or cause to move back and forth or from side to side while suspended or on an axis
example: The monkey loves to swing from tree to tree.

meaning: The sensation of flavor perceived in the mouth and throat on contact with a substance
example: This soup has a very spicy taste.

meaning: To show or explain to someone how to do something
example: My older brother is going to teach me how to ride a bicycle.

meaning: To pull or rip something apart or to pieces with force
example: Be careful not to tear the thin paper.

meaning: To make fun of or attempt to provoke a person or animal in a playful way
example: It is not nice to tease others about their mistakes.

meaning: A system for transmitting voices over a distance using wire or radio, by converting acoustic vibrations to electrical signals
example: I will use the telephone to call my grandmother.

meaning: To communicate information, facts, or news to someone in spoken or written words
example: Please tell me the true story of what happened.

meaning: A procedure intended to establish the quality, performance, or reliability of something, especially before it is taken into widespread use
example: We have an English spelling test every Friday.

meaning: To express gratitude to someone, especially by saying 'Thank you'
example: Remember to thank your friend for the birthday present.

meaning: To lightly touch or prod a person or a part of the body in a way that causes itching and often laughter
example: My baby brother laughs when I tickle his tummy.

meaning: To attach or fasten someone or something with string or similar cord
example: I learned how to tie my own shoelaces today.

meaning: To come so close to an object as to be or come into contact with it
example: The sign in the museum said 'Do not touch the exhibits'.

meaning: The action of buying and selling goods and services

meaning: To teach a person or animal a particular skill or type of behavior through practice and instruction
example: We need to train our new dog to sit and stay.

meaning: To behave towards or deal with in a certain way
example: You must treat animals with kindness and respect.

meaning: A cunning or skillful act or scheme intended to deceive or outwit someone
example: The magician showed us a very clever card trick.

meaning: To catch one's foot on something and stumble or fall
example: Be careful not to trip over the loose rug.

meaning: Firm belief in the reliability, truth, ability, or strength of someone or something

meaning: To make an attempt or effort to do something

meaning: To move or cause to move in a circular direction wholly or partly around an axis or point

meaning: To form into a bent, curling, or distorted shape

meaning: To write something on a typewriter or computer by pressing the keys

meaning: To take, hold, or deploy something as a means of accomplishing a purpose or achieving a result

meaning: To stay where one is or delay action until a particular time or until something else happens
example: We have to wait for the green man before crossing the road.

meaning: To emerge or cause to emerge from a state of sleep
example: I use an alarm clock to wake me up in the morning.

meaning: To move at a regular and fairly slow pace by lifting and setting down each foot in turn
example: I usually walk to school with my neighbour.

meaning: To walk or move in a leisurely, casual, or aimless way
example: We decided to wander around the shopping mall for an hour.

meaning: To inform someone in advance of an impending or possible danger, problem, or other unpleasant situation
example: The weather forecast warned us about the heavy storm.

meaning: To clean with water and, typically, soap or detergent
example: Please wash your hands with soap before eating.

meaning: To use or expend carelessly, extravagantly, or to no purpose
example: We should not waste food; take only what you can eat.

meaning: To look at or observe attentively over a period of time
example: We sat down to watch a movie on television.

meaning: To move one's hand to and fro in greeting or as a signal
example: I always wave goodbye to my parents when they drop me off.

meaning: To find out how heavy someone or something is, typically using scales
example: The nurse will weigh you during your health checkup.

meaning: An instance or manner of greeting someone
example: We gave a warm welcome to the new student in our class.

meaning: To emit a clear, high-pitched sound by forcing breath through a small hole between one's lips or teeth
example: The PE teacher will blow his whistle to start the race.

meaning: To clean or dry something by rubbing its surface with a cloth, a piece of paper, or one's hand
example: Please wipe the table with a wet cloth.

meaning: To feel or express a strong desire or hope for something that is not easily attainable
example: Make a wish before you blow out your birthday candles!

meaning: To desire or be curious to know something
example: I wonder what we are having for lunch today.

meaning: Activity involving mental or physical effort done in order to achieve a purpose or result
example: We have to work together to finish the group project.

meaning: To give way to anxiety or unease; allow one's mind to dwell on difficulty or troubles
example: Do not worry too much about the test; just do your best.

meaning: To cover or enclose in paper or soft material
example: Mother helped me wrap the birthday present in colourful paper.

meaning: To destroy or severely damage a structure, vehicle, or similar
example: The storm caused the ship to wreck on the rocks.

meaning: To involuntarily open one's mouth wide and inhale deeply due to tiredness or boredom
